summary: S1 audit + design-lock. Ran cross-provider consensus (devil's-advocate two-pass, gemini-pro + gpt-5-4, both passes) on all 11 open design questions; 8 of 11 dispositions changed. Locked: pure-JS hot-path drift scan (no ai_router dependency, no network) so stale/no-router consumers stay protected; declarative advisory off-hot-path schema-current.json manifest (no shell strings); check_migrations detect-only (--apply cut), bulk upgrade = two existing migrators in sequence; Feature 2 ships both an ai_router resolver and a minimal extension quick-input command; NNN- prefix required for new sets. Produced proposal.md + verdict.md + per-pass artifacts; scope-locked spec.md; journaled consensus ($0.2829 of the $10 set NTE).

reason.specifics: S2 (manifest + detect-only check_migrations CLI) is straightforward Python implementation off the S1-locked verdict; same orchestrator keeps the design context warm and the audit rationale at hand.
